title: targets/<product>/link-map.yaml
description: Per-product install link map. Drives `agent-runtime install` plan generation: which build / target paths land where under the product runtime home, what gets symlinked, what gets copied, what edits a config file via a managed block, and what existing files must be backed up before replacement. Overlay merge for link maps is per-entry replace (keyed by `id`). The four entry kinds are symlinked-file (build/), plugin-manifest-copy (targets/), managed-block, and backed-up-on-replace.

entries.description: Install entries in execution order. `id` is unique within the file and is the key for overlay merge `per-entry replace` semantics.

id.description: Stable identifier (`<plugin>.<surface>` recommended). Used by overlay merge to replace a single entry without touching its siblings.

source.description: Path relative to the source root (the agent-runtime-kit checkout). Required for symlinked-file / plugin-manifest-copy / backed-up-on-replace. Forbidden for managed-block (which carries inline content via `body_template` or a `surface` keyed to a renderer fragment).

destination.description: Path relative to the product's `live_home` (resolved from manifests/runtime-roots.yaml). For managed-block entries this is the file the block lives in.

recursive.description: When `kind=symlinked-file` and `source` resolves to a directory, `recursive: true` installs the tree symlink-by-symlink; the default `false` installs the directory itself as one symlink.

surface.description: Required for `kind=managed-block`. Passed to the `ManagedBlock::new(surface, ...)` helper. ASCII alphanumeric / `-` / `_` only.

comment_style.description: Required for `kind=managed-block`. Selects the marker prefix the managed-block helper applies (`#` for TOML, `//` for JSONC).

body_template.description: Required for `kind=managed-block`. Inline content placed between the markers. Only static bodies are supported today; templating (env vars, render fragments) is future work.
